config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan mengorbankan individu secara berlebihan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any claim that the "common good" justifies overriding basic liberties, because history shows that governments and majorities can abuse that power very easily. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since laws against violence, fraud, and reckless harm exist to protect everyone. To me, the right balance is that society can limit individual freedom only when it is necessary, narrow, and clearly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju jika kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara otomatis, karena di Indonesia sendiri alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran dan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Memang benar masyarakat butuh ketertiban, keamanan, dan solidaritas, tetapi itu tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menginjak hak dasar orang, terutama kelompok yang lemah atau minoritas. Yang masuk akal menurut saya adalah kepentingan bersama boleh membatasi hak individu hanya bila benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan negara atau mayoritas yang terlalu mudah menekan warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, I am very skeptical of any broad “common good” argument, because history shows that majorities and governments often use it to excuse abuse, discrimination, or censorship. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited; society can impose narrow limits for real public safety, fraud prevention, and similar harms, but those limits have to be necessary and tightly controlled. The burden should always be on society to prove why overriding a right is justified, not on the individual to prove why their liberty deserves protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup penyalahgunaan kuasa, membungkam kritik, atau menekan kelompok yang lemah. Yang saya anggap benar adalah kepentingan bersama boleh membatasi hak individu hanya kalau benar-benar perlu, jelas manfaatnya, dan diawasi ketat supaya tidak jadi alat mayoritas atau negara untuk semena-mena.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general principle. In the United States, I see how quickly “the common good” can become a blank check for censorship, discrimination, or government overreach, so I want the burden of proof to stay very high before any right is limited. The other side is right that rights cannot be absolute in every situation, because public safety and preventing direct harm do matter, but those exceptions should be narrow and carefully justified. If society is allowed to outrank the individual too easily, the people who lose first are usually minorities, dissenters, and anyone with less power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
